title: Clone assassin

{{Quote|You haven't seen anything as fast as I am.|An unidentified clone assassin|<ref name="ROTSvg" />}}
'''Clone assassins''' were a specialized class of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] created by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. These [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] were commissioned by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], through a special order during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The assassins were trained specifically to [[Death/Legends|eliminate]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] by utilizing [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]], a form of [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]]. The clones displayed greatly enhanced speed as well as an aptitude for anticipating strikes and the ability to resist [[Mind trick/Legends|Jedi mind tricks]].

[[Assassin/Legends|Assassins]] were fully put in use following the activation of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], a measure trained into all clone troops that commanded them to kill their Jedi superiors. A [[unit]] of the clone variant was sent by Palpatine to assist in an [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|assault]] on the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], which took place in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. There, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the leader of the assault, along with the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], cleared the Temple of all Jedi. Vader activated a [[Jedi beacon/Legends|beacon]], inside the [[Jedi Temple communication center/Legends|Jedi Temple communication center]], designed to contact all Jedi across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and sent a message ordering all Jedi to return to the Temple, where a large [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] of [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] troops was waiting to eliminate each one that did so. The squad charged with defending the beacon was killed by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who was attempting to alter the message to ward any surviving Jedi away, when they attacked him and were rebuffed.

==History==
{{Quote|Stay and protect the beacon. Guard it with your lives.|Darth Vader to a squad of clone assassins|<ref name="ROTSvg" />}}
[[File:Clone_Assassin.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Clone assassins were stationed in the Jedi Temple in the wake of Operation: Knightfall.]]
[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] were specially commissioned by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], actually the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, as an addition to the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] through the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Palpatine justified the need for these troops in the legislation as a way to combat [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] if they ever decided to rebel against the Senate and the Republic.<ref name="TCWCG" />

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the final [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the Clone Wars, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and friend of the Chancellor, swore fealty to Sidious' [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings and became his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], taking the name Darth Vader. Afterward, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] sent a [[unit]] of clone assassins to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], with Vader and the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], to [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|exterminate the Jedi inside]].<ref name="TCWCG" /> Amidst of the battle inside the Temple, Vader assigned a squad of six assassins to protect the [[Jedi Temple communication center/Legends|Jedi Temple communication center]], where he had activated a [[Jedi beacon/Legends|beacon]] that transmitted to all Jedi in the galaxy, informing them that the war was over and that they were to return to the Temple. A large garrison of clone troops was left in the Temple to intercept and eliminate any returning Jedi.<ref name="ROTSvg" />

After receiving the beacon's message and hearing of the attack on the Jedi Temple, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], deeming it an obvious trap, decided to [[Infiltration of the Jedi Temple/Legends|infiltrate]] the Temple in an effort to stop the signal's broadcast. Kenobi entered the Communication Room and was cornered by the squad of assassins Vader had stationed there, though he successfully rebuffed them all.<ref name="ROTSvg" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|I'm going to eviscerate you!|An unidentified clone assassin|<ref name="ROTSvg" />}}
[[File:CloneAssassinCA-ROTSGG.png|thumb|right|180px|Clone assassins wore an armor variant outfitted with vibro-blades.]]
Clone assassins were outfitted in a standardized variation of the [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II clone trooper armor]] to protect them in battle. Wearing this full-body [[Armor/Legends|armor]], they were still able to perform incredibly dexterous moves to avoid attacks with no obstruction to their movements. The armor had two twin [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibro-blades]] attached to the elbows which extended their reach and increased the lethality of their attacks. These blades spun out from a neutral position and could be locked in place<ref name="ROTSvg" /> to either follow the troopers' upper arms or their forearms as they swung at their opponents. The assassins wore parted [[Kama/Legends|''kamas'']], which were kept on by a belt with two storage pouches on each hip.<ref name="ROTSvg" /> They also carried [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]].<ref name="TCWCG" />

==Applications==
{{Quote|Clone assassins. Very deadly.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="ROTSvg" />}}
Assassins were commissioned and created for the sole purpose of combating Jedi on equal terms. They were trained to use the martial art of [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]],<ref name="ROTSvg" /> a form of [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]] that was created specifically for defeating Jedi,<ref name="SoE">''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> in conjunction with twin vibro-blades on their arms. They could easily dodge or parry most strikes from [[Melee/Legends|melee]] weapons, such as [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], and were also able to withstand [[Mind trick/Legends|Jedi mind tricks]] without flinching.<ref name="ROTSvg" /> A [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of clone assassins could easily defeat a Jedi Knight though were not a true match for a Jedi Master. In addition to their unarmed combat, the assassins were also familiar with the use of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] technology and carried blaster pistols on their persons.<ref name="TCWCG" /> They were used to assist in the cleansing of the Jedi Temple and, later, to defend the temple beacon room from Jedi.<ref name="ROTSvg" />

==Behind the scenes==
This clone trooper classification first appeared as an enemy character in the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|adaptation]]. There is a bonus level of the game where Yoda is confronted in the Temple by several squads of clone troops and among them is a squad of six assassins, though the [[canon]]icity of this encounter is ambiguous. Concept art from the game depicts a clone assassin with dark armor as opposed to the white and blue seen in the game.<ref name="ROTSvg" />

The assassins are voiced by Raphael Sbarge who also voiced Carth Onasi in Knights of the Old Republic, and RC-1262 Scorch in Republic Commando.

In the second Jedi Temple level of Obi-Wan's storyline in the GBA/DS versions of the game adaptation, Obi-Wan ends up fighting a clone trooper that had similar colors to the Clone Assassin, although his method of attacking was closer to the Heavy Weapons Specialist, which was using a launcher to fire several grenades into the air to trap Obi-Wan.
